title: Bind Acceptance Criteria to Executed Evidence
problemStatement: Summary validation confirms that verification commands ran, but it cannot prove which Acceptance criterion each command supports, whether the referenced test exists and ran, or whether a high-risk change received human review.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: v2 Contract with identified Acceptance criteria and complete executed evidence
Evidence references missing or unexecuted tests
High-risk or bug-fix Acceptance lacks required review/failure evidence
Historic v1 archive remains readable without v2 Acceptance mapping

outOfScope: product source code outside Summary/Contract governance

sources.reason: Work Item 3 objective, compatibility, and required evidence scenarios.
Existing Acceptance signal policy and compatibility behavior.
Summary schema and verification evidence validation entry point.
Finish-time Summary evidence generation and readiness promotion.
Policy source for Acceptance ID/evidence and high-risk review requirements.
Active Contract for this Work Item.

riskAssessment.reason: The change tightens finish evidence requirements and must preserve historic v1 readability while preventing unsupported Acceptance claims.

executionDecision.reason: The user explicitly authorized the implementation plan and normal repository workflow.

preReviewWarnings: Review v2 Acceptance ID parsing, evidence execution binding, high-risk human review, and v1 compatibility.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: v2 Acceptance criteria use stable A<n>: identifiers, and Summary acceptanceEvidence maps every ID exactly once.
Every Acceptance evidence item references an existing repository-relative test path, locator, and one or more Verification IDs whose recorded result is passed.
Missing IDs, missing mappings, duplicate mappings, missing test paths, unexecuted Verification, and bug-fix mappings without failure scenarios are rejected.
High-risk v2 Work Items require an explicit human review marker in acceptanceEvidence; low/medium risk behavior remains compatible.
The mapping is retained in Summary/Archive evidence, historic Contract v1 remains readable, and the focused tests plus make quality pass.
guidelines: Acceptance evidence must be explicit, repository-relative, and traceable to executed Verification.
Keep Contract v1 archive validation read-only compatible; apply strict Acceptance mapping only to v2 active Work Items.
Require human review evidence only for high-risk Work Items, without hard-coding a universal two-person policy.
Do not claim an Acceptance is complete from command presence alone.

intent.problem: Verification results currently prove that checks ran but not which Acceptance criterion they support.
constraints: Use A<n>: IDs in v2 Acceptance strings to remain compatible with the existing Contract checker.
Historic v1 archive records must continue to validate without new mapping fields.
Evidence paths must be repository-relative and must not expose machine-specific paths.
High-risk review is an explicit marker, not a hard-coded organization policy.
intent.rationale: A small evidence mapping makes each Acceptance auditable without changing the existing registered Verification command model.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Explicit user authorization covers the plan's governance policy, documentation, tests, branch, PR, merge, and cleanup changes.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
